## 执行要求

请按照 planner prompt 中的规则执行。

你需要输出：

- 一个完整 manifest JSON。
- full 模式下，所有讲义 `action` 为 `"new"`。
- incremental 模式下，根据 diff 判断每篇讲义的 `action`。

不要写任何 Markdown 讲义文件。

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

# Crossbeam [![Build Status](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/workflows/CI/badge.svg)]( https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/actions) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT_OR_Apache--2.0-blue.svg)]( https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam#license) [![Cargo](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/crossbeam.svg)]( https://crates.io/crates/crossbeam) [![Documentation](https://docs.rs/crossbeam/badge.svg)]( https://docs.rs/crossbeam) [![Rust 1.74+](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-1.74+-lightgray.svg)]( https://www.rust-lang.org) [![chat](https://img.shields.io/discord/569610676205781012.svg?logo=discord)](https://discord.com/invite/JXYwgWZ) This crate provides a set of tools for concurrent programming: #### Atomics * [`AtomicCell`], a thread-safe mutable memory location.(no_std) * [`AtomicConsume`], for reading from primitive atomic types with "consume" ordering.(no_std) #### Data structures * [`deque`], work-stealing deques for building task schedulers. * [`ArrayQueue`], a bounded MPMC queue that allocates a fixed-capacity buffer on construction.(alloc) * [`SegQueue`], an unbounded MPMC queue that allocates small buffers, segments, on demand.(alloc) #### Memory management * [`epoch`], an epoch-based garbage collector.(alloc) #### Thread synchronization * [`channel`], multi-producer multi-consumer channels for message passing. * [`Parker`], a thread parking primitive. * [`ShardedLock`], a sharded reader-writer lock with fast concurrent reads. * [`WaitGroup`], for synchronizing the beginning or end of some computation. #### Utilities * [`Backoff`], for exponential backoff in spin loops.(no_std) * [`CachePadded`], for padding and aligning a value to the length of a cache line.(no_std) * [`scope`], for spawning threads that borrow local variables from the stack. *Features marked with (no_std) can be used in `no_std` environments.*
*Features marked with (alloc) can be used in `no_std` environments, but only if `alloc` feature is enabled.* [`AtomicCell`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/atomic/struct.AtomicCell.html [`AtomicConsume`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/atomic/trait.AtomicConsume.html [`deque`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/deque/index.html [`ArrayQueue`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/queue/struct.ArrayQueue.html [`SegQueue`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/queue/struct.SegQueue.html [`channel`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/channel/index.html [`Parker`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/sync/struct.Parker.html [`ShardedLock`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/sync/struct.ShardedLock.html [`WaitGroup`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/sync/struct.WaitGroup.html [`epoch`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/epoch/index.html [`Backoff`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/utils/struct.Backoff.html [`CachePadded`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/utils/struct.CachePadded.html [`scope`]: https://docs.rs/crossbeam/latest/crossbeam/fn.scope.html ## Crates The main `crossbeam` crate just [re-exports](src/lib.rs) tools from smaller subcrates: * [`crossbeam-channel`](crossbeam-channel) provides multi-producer multi-consumer channels for message passing. * [`crossbeam-deque`](crossbeam-deque) provides work-stealing deques, which are primarily intended for building task schedulers. * [`crossbeam-epoch`](crossbeam-epoch) provides epoch-based garbage collection for building concurrent data structures. * [`crossbeam-queue`](crossbeam-queue) provides concurrent queues that can be shared among threads. * [`crossbeam-utils`](crossbeam-utils) provides atomics, synchronization primitives, scoped threads, and other utilities. There is one more experimental subcrate that is not yet included in `crossbeam`: * [`crossbeam-skiplist`](crossbeam-skiplist) provides concurrent maps and sets based on lock-free skip lists. ## Usage Add this to your `Cargo.toml`: ```toml [dependencies] crossbeam = "0.8" ``` ## Compatibility Crossbeam supports stable Rust releases going back at least six months, and every time the minimum supported Rust version is increased, a new minor version is released. Currently, the minimum supported Rust version is 1.74. ## Contributing Crossbeam welcomes contribution from everyone in the form of suggestions, bug reports, pull requests, and feedback. 💛 If you need ideas for contribution, there are several ways to get started: * Found a bug or have a feature request? [Submit an issue](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/issues/new)! * Issues and PRs labeled with [feedback wanted](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/issues?utf8=%E2%9C%93&q=is%3Aopen+sort%3Aupdated-desc+label%3A%22feedback+wanted%22+) need feedback from users and contributors. * Issues labeled with [good first issue](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+sort%3Aupdated-desc+label%3A%22good+first+issue%22) are relatively easy starter issues. #### RFCs We also have the [RFCs](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs) repository for more high-level discussion, which is the place where we brainstorm ideas and propose substantial changes to Crossbeam. You are welcome to participate in any open [issues](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aopen+sort%3Aupdated-desc) or [pull requests](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs/pulls?q=is%3Apr+is%3Aopen+sort%3Aupdated-desc). #### Learning resources If you'd like to learn more about concurrency and non-blocking data structures, there's a list of learning resources in our [wiki](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs/wiki), which includes relevant blog posts, papers, videos, and other similar projects. Another good place to visit is [merged RFCs](https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/rfcs/tree/HEAD/text). They contain elaborate descriptions and rationale for features we've introduced to Crossbeam, but keep in mind that some of the written information is now out of date. #### Conduct The Crossbeam project adheres to the [Rust Code of Conduct](https://www.rust-lang.org/policies/code-of-conduct). This describes the minimum behavior expected from all contributors. ## License Licensed under either of * Apache License, Version 2.0 ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE) or http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) * MIT license ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT) or http://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) at your option. Some Crossbeam subcrates have additional licensing notices. [package]
name = "crossbeam"
# When publishing a new version:
# - Update CHANGELOG.md
# - Update README.md (when increasing major or minor version)
# - Run './tools/publish.sh crossbeam <version>'
version = "0.8.4"
edition = "2021"
# NB: Sync with msrv badge and "Compatibility" section in README.md
rust-version = "1.74"
license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
repository = "https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam"
homepage = "https://github.com/crossbeam-rs/crossbeam"
description = "Tools for concurrent programming"
keywords = ["atomic", "garbage", "non-blocking", "lock-free", "rcu"]
categories = ["concurrency", "memory-management", "data-structures", "no-std"]
exclude = ["/.*", "/ci", "/tools"]

[package.metadata.docs.rs]
targets = ["x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu"]

[package.metadata.cargo_check_external_types]
# The following are external types that are allowed to be exposed in our public API.
allowed_external_types = [
    "crossbeam_channel",
    "crossbeam_channel::*",
    "crossbeam_deque",
    "crossbeam_epoch",
    "crossbeam_queue",
    "crossbeam_utils::*",
]

[features]
default = ["std"]

# Enable to use APIs that require `std`.
# This is enabled by default.
std = [
    "alloc",
    "crossbeam-channel/std",
    "crossbeam-deque/std",
    "crossbeam-epoch/std",
    "crossbeam-queue/std",
    "crossbeam-utils/std",
]

# Enable to use APIs that require `alloc`.
# This is enabled by default and also enabled if the `std` feature is enabled.
alloc = ["crossbeam-epoch/alloc", "crossbeam-queue/alloc"]

[dependencies]
crossbeam-channel = { version = "0.5.10", path = "crossbeam-channel", default-features = false, optional = true }
crossbeam-deque = { version = "0.8.4", path = "crossbeam-deque", default-features = false, optional = true }
crossbeam-epoch = { version = "0.9.17", path = "crossbeam-epoch", default-features = false, optional = true }
crossbeam-queue = { version = "0.3.10", path = "crossbeam-queue", default-features = false, optional = true }
crossbeam-utils = { version = "0.8.18", path = "crossbeam-utils", default-features = false, features = ["atomic"] }

[dev-dependencies]

[lints]
workspace = true

[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = [
    ".",
    "crossbeam-channel",
    "crossbeam-channel/benchmarks",
    "crossbeam-deque",
    "crossbeam-epoch",
    "crossbeam-queue",
    "crossbeam-skiplist",
    "crossbeam-utils",
]

[workspace.lints.rust]
missing_debug_implementations = "warn"
rust_2018_idioms = "warn"
single_use_lifetimes = "warn"
unexpected_cfgs = { level = "warn", check-cfg = [
    'cfg(crossbeam_loom)',
    'cfg(crossbeam_sanitize)',
    'cfg(gha_macos_runner)',
] }
unreachable_pub = "warn"
# unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn = "warn" # Set at crate-level instead since https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/pull/100081 is not available on MSRV
[workspace.lints.clippy]
# Suppress buggy or noisy clippy lints
bool_assert_comparison = { level = "allow", priority = 1 }
declare_interior_mutable_const = { level = "allow", priority = 1 } # https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/issues/7665
incompatible_msrv = { level = "allow", priority = 1 } # buggy: doesn't consider cfg, https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/issues/12280, https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/issues/12257#issuecomment-2093667187
lint_groups_priority = { level = "allow", priority = 1 } # https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/issues/12920
manual_map = { level = "allow", priority = 1 }
manual_range_contains = { level = "allow", priority = 1 } # https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-clippy/issues/6455#issuecomment-1225966395
type_complexity = { level = "allow", priority = 1 }

1	//! Tools for concurrent programming.
2	//!
3	//! ## Atomics
4	//!
5	//! * [`AtomicCell`], a thread-safe mutable memory location.
6	//! * [`AtomicConsume`], for reading from primitive atomic types with "consume" ordering.
7	//!
8	//! ## Data structures
9	//!
10	//! * [`deque`], work-stealing deques for building task schedulers.
11	//! * [`ArrayQueue`], a bounded MPMC queue that allocates a fixed-capacity buffer on construction.
12	//! * [`SegQueue`], an unbounded MPMC queue that allocates small buffers, segments, on demand.
13	//!
14	//! ## Memory management
15	//!
16	//! * [`epoch`], an epoch-based garbage collector.
17	//!
18	//! ## Thread synchronization
19	//!
20	//! * [`channel`], multi-producer multi-consumer channels for message passing.
21	//! * [`Parker`], a thread parking primitive.
22	//! * [`ShardedLock`], a sharded reader-writer lock with fast concurrent reads.
23	//! * [`WaitGroup`], for synchronizing the beginning or end of some computation.
24	//!
25	//! ## Utilities
26	//!
27	//! * [`Backoff`], for exponential backoff in spin loops.
28	//! * [`CachePadded`], for padding and aligning a value to the length of a cache line.
29	//! * [`scope`], for spawning threads that borrow local variables from the stack.
30	//!
31	//! [`AtomicCell`]: atomic::AtomicCell
32	//! [`AtomicConsume`]: atomic::AtomicConsume
33	//! [`ArrayQueue`]: queue::ArrayQueue
34	//! [`SegQueue`]: queue::SegQueue
35	//! [`Parker`]: sync::Parker
36	//! [`ShardedLock`]: sync::ShardedLock
37	//! [`WaitGroup`]: sync::WaitGroup
38	//! [`Backoff`]: utils::Backoff
39	//! [`CachePadded`]: utils::CachePadded
40	
41	#![no_std]
42	#![doc(test(
43	    no_crate_inject,
44	    attr(allow(dead_code, unused_assignments, unused_variables))
45	))]
46	#![warn(
47	    missing_docs,
48	    unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn,
49	    clippy::alloc_instead_of_core,
50	    clippy::std_instead_of_alloc,
51	    clippy::std_instead_of_core
52	)]
53	
54	#[cfg(feature = "std")]
55	extern crate std;
56	
57	pub use crossbeam_utils::atomic;
58	
59	pub mod utils {
60	    //! Miscellaneous utilities.
61	    //!
62	    //! * [`Backoff`], for exponential backoff in spin loops.
63	    //! * [`CachePadded`], for padding and aligning a value to the length of a cache line.
64	
65	    pub use crossbeam_utils::{Backoff, CachePadded};
66	}
67	
68	#[cfg(feature = "std")]
69	#[cfg(not(crossbeam_loom))]
70	pub use crossbeam_utils::thread::{self, scope};
71	#[cfg(feature = "std")]
72	#[doc(inline)]
73	pub use {
74	    crossbeam_channel as channel, crossbeam_channel::select, crossbeam_deque as deque,
75	    crossbeam_utils::sync,
76	};
77	#[cfg(feature = "alloc")]
78	#[doc(inline)]
79	pub use {crossbeam_epoch as epoch, crossbeam_queue as queue};
80

- `swap` (line 424): Returns previous `Shared<'g, T>`

## STRUCTURAL MAP FOR CROSSBEAM CRATES

### **crossbeam-deque**

**1. src/lib.rs** — Public API
- **Worker<T>** (line 197): Owner queue, FIFO/LIFO flavors
- **Stealer<T>** (line 574): Shared handle for stealing
- **Injector<T>** (line 1332): Global FIFO queue for task injection
- **Steal<T>** enum (line 2085): `Empty`, `Success(T)`, `Retry`

---

**2. src/deque.rs** — Core Chase-Lev Implementation

**Inner types:**
- **Inner** (line 114): Shared state with `front: AtomicIsize`, `back: AtomicIsize`, `buffer: Atomic<Buffer<T>>`
- **Buffer** (line 29): Circular buffer holding tasks; `cap` is power-of-two, wraps via `index & (cap - 1)`

**Chase-Lev deque mechanics:**
- **Indices**: `front` (steal side), `back` (owner side), both `AtomicIsize` wrapping on overflow
- **Circular buffer**: `Buffer::at(index)` computes `index & (self.cap - 1)` (line 69)
- **Buffer growth**: `resize()` (line 291) allocates new buffer, copies tasks [front, back), swaps via epoch-based reclamation
- **FIFO vs LIFO**: Controlled by `Flavor` enum (line 148). Worker `push()` always writes at `back`; `pop()` direction depends on flavor:
  - FIFO: `pop()` from `front` (increment front)
  - LIFO: `pop()` from `back-1` (decrement back, read, optionally restore)

**Key operations:**
- **push()** (line 399): Write task at `back`, fence, increment `back`. Grows buffer if full.
- **pop()** (line 450):
  - FIFO: CAS-increment `front`, read task, shrink if ¼ capacity
  - LIFO: decrement `back`, read, handle race with last task via CAS on `front`
- **steal()** (line 641): Load `front`, epoch pin, read task at `front`, CAS-increment `front`. Returns `Retry` if CAS fails or buffer swapped.

**Stealing protocol:**
- Stealer loads buffer and task at `front`, then CAS-increments `front`
- **Steal::Retry**: CAS failed or concurrent buffer swap detected
- **Steal::Empty**: `back - front <= 0`
- **Steal::Success**: CAS succeeded, return task

**CachePadded usage:** `Inner::front`, `Inner::back`, `Inner::buffer` all wrapped (lines 116-122) to prevent false sharing.

**3. src/lib.rs** — Public API

- **SegQueue** (line 161 in seg_queue.rs): Unbounded MPMC segmented queue

**4. src/array_queue.rs** — Bounded MPMC Ring Buffer

**Core structure:**
- **ArrayQueue** (line 52):
  - `head: CachePadded<AtomicUsize>` — read position
  - `tail: CachePadded<AtomicUsize>` — write position
  - `buffer: Box<[Slot<T>]>` — fixed slots
  - `one_lap: usize` — power-of-two ≥ capacity, used for modulo

**Slot layout (line 18):**
- `stamp: AtomicUsize` — sync state (lap + index)
- `value: UnsafeCell<MaybeUninit<T>>` — data

**Epoch-style indexing:**
- Indices are `{lap, index}` packed into `usize`
- Lower bits = index into buffer; upper bits = lap (generation counter)
- `one_lap` = smallest power-of-two > capacity (line 117)
- Prevents ABA: stale slot with old lap won't match current stamp

**push()** (line 127):
- Loop: deconstruct `tail` into `lap` and `index`
- Check slot's `stamp == tail` to verify slot is writable
- CAS `tail` to `new_tail`
- On success, write value and update `stamp = tail + 1`

**pop()** (line 318):
- Loop: deconstruct `head` into `lap` and `index`
- Check `head + 1 == stamp` to verify slot is readable
- CAS `head` to `new`
- On success, read value and set `stamp = head + one_lap` (next lap)

---

**5. src/seg_queue.rs** — Unbounded Segmented Queue

**Core structure:**
- **SegQueue** (line 161):
  - `head: CachePadded<Position<T>>` — read end
  - `tail: CachePadded<Position<T>>` — write end
  - Each `Position` has `index: AtomicUsize` and `block: AtomicPtr<Block<T>>`

**Segment layout (lines 56, 35):**
- `Block` holds `BLOCK_CAP = 31` slots (`LAP - 1`, where `LAP = 32`)
- Each slot has `state: AtomicUsize` with bits `WRITE=1, READ=2, DESTROY=4`
- `next: AtomicPtr<Block<T>>` links blocks

**Index encoding (line 30):**
- `SHIFT = 1` reserves lower bit for metadata
- `HAS_NEXT = 1` bit signals more blocks exist
- Index = `(lap * LAP + offset) << SHIFT | HAS_NEXT?`

**push()** (line 214):
- Loop: calculate `offset = (tail >> SHIFT) % LAP`
- Pre-allocate next block if at `BLOCK_CAP - 1`
- CAS `tail.index` forward
- On success: if at block end, install next block; write value into slot and set `WRITE` bit

**pop()** (line 364):
- Loop: calculate `offset = (head >> SHIFT) % LAP`
- Check empty: `head >> SHIFT == tail >> SHIFT` → `None`
- CAS `head.index` forward
- On success: if at block end, move to next block; `wait_write()` on slot, read value, set `READ` bit; trigger `Block::destroy()` if needed

**Segment retirement (lines 105-127):**
- `Block::destroy()` sets `DESTROY` bit and checks if any slot still in use (`READ == 0`)
- If all slots read, deallocates block; otherwise, defers to thread that last reads a slot

## STRUCTURAL MAP FOR CROSSBEAM CRATES

### **crossbeam-deque**

**1. src/lib.rs** — Public API
- **Worker<T>** (line 197): Owner queue, FIFO/LIFO flavors
- **Stealer<T>** (line 574): Shared handle for stealing
- **Injector<T>** (line 1332): Global FIFO queue for task injection
- **Steal<T>** enum (line 2085): `Empty`, `Success(T)`, `Retry`

---

**2. src/deque.rs** — Core Chase-Lev Implementation

**Inner types:**
- **Inner** (line 114): Shared state with `front: AtomicIsize`, `back: AtomicIsize`, `buffer: Atomic<Buffer<T>>`
- **Buffer** (line 29): Circular buffer holding tasks; `cap` is power-of-two, wraps via `index & (cap - 1)`

**Chase-Lev deque mechanics:**
- **Indices**: `front` (steal side), `back` (owner side), both `AtomicIsize` wrapping on overflow
- **Circular buffer**: `Buffer::at(index)` computes `index & (self.cap - 1)` (line 69)
- **Buffer growth**: `resize()` (line 291) allocates new buffer, copies tasks [front, back), swaps via epoch-based reclamation
- **FIFO vs LIFO**: Controlled by `Flavor` enum (line 148). Worker `push()` always writes at `back`; `pop()` direction depends on flavor:
  - FIFO: `pop()` from `front` (increment front)
  - LIFO: `pop()` from `back-1` (decrement back, read, optionally restore)

**Key operations:**
- **push()** (line 399): Write task at `back`, fence, increment `back`. Grows buffer if full.
- **pop()** (line 450):
  - FIFO: CAS-increment `front`, read task, shrink if ¼ capacity
  - LIFO: decrement `back`, read, handle race with last task via CAS on `front`
- **steal()** (line 641): Load `front`, epoch pin, read task at `front`, CAS-increment `front`. Returns `Retry` if CAS fails or buffer swapped.

**Stealing protocol:**
- Stealer loads buffer and task at `front`, then CAS-increments `front`
- **Steal::Retry**: CAS failed or concurrent buffer swap detected
- **Steal::Empty**: `back - front <= 0`
- **Steal::Success**: CAS succeeded, return task

**CachePadded usage:** `Inner::front`, `Inner::back`, `Inner::buffer` all wrapped (lines 116-122) to prevent false sharing.

---

### **crossbeam-queue**

**3. src/lib.rs** — Public API
- **ArrayQueue** (line 29 in array_queue.rs): Bounded MPMC ring buffer
- **SegQueue** (line 161 in seg_queue.rs): Unbounded MPMC segmented queue

---

**4. src/array_queue.rs** — Bounded MPMC Ring Buffer

**Core structure:**
- **ArrayQueue** (line 52):
  - `head: CachePadded<AtomicUsize>` — read position
  - `tail: CachePadded<AtomicUsize>` — write position
  - `buffer: Box<[Slot<T>]>` — fixed slots
  - `one_lap: usize` — power-of-two ≥ capacity, used for modulo

**Slot layout (line 18):**
- `stamp: AtomicUsize` — sync state (lap + index)
- `value: UnsafeCell<MaybeUninit<T>>` — data

**Epoch-style indexing:**
- Indices are `{lap, index}` packed into `usize`
- Lower bits = index into buffer; upper bits = lap (generation counter)
- `one_lap` = smallest power-of-two > capacity (line 117)
- Prevents ABA: stale slot with old lap won't match current stamp

**push()** (line 127):
- Loop: deconstruct `tail` into `lap` and `index`
- Check slot's `stamp == tail` to verify slot is writable
- CAS `tail` to `new_tail`
- On success, write value and update `stamp = tail + 1`

**pop()** (line 318):
- Loop: deconstruct `head` into `lap` and `index`
- Check `head + 1 == stamp` to verify slot is readable
- CAS `head` to `new`
- On success, read value and set `stamp = head + one_lap` (next lap)

---

**5. src/seg_queue.rs** — Unbounded Segmented Queue

**Core structure:**
- **SegQueue** (line 161):
  - `head: CachePadded<Position<T>>` — read end
  - `tail: CachePadded<Position<T>>` — write end
  - Each `Position` has `index: AtomicUsize` and `block: AtomicPtr<Block<T>>`

**Segment layout (lines 56, 35):**
- `Block` holds `BLOCK_CAP = 31` slots (`LAP - 1`, where `LAP = 32`)
- Each slot has `state: AtomicUsize` with bits `WRITE=1, READ=2, DESTROY=4`
- `next: AtomicPtr<Block<T>>` links blocks

**Index encoding (line 30):**
- `SHIFT = 1` reserves lower bit for metadata
- `HAS_NEXT = 1` bit signals more blocks exist
- Index = `(lap * LAP + offset) << SHIFT | HAS_NEXT?`

**push()** (line 214):
- Loop: calculate `offset = (tail >> SHIFT) % LAP`
- Pre-allocate next block if at `BLOCK_CAP - 1`
- CAS `tail.index` forward
- On success: if at block end, install next block; write value into slot and set `WRITE` bit

**pop()** (line 364):
- Loop: calculate `offset = (head >> SHIFT) % LAP`
- Check empty: `head >> SHIFT == tail >> SHIFT` → `None`
- CAS `head.index` forward
- On success: if at block end, move to next block; `wait_write()` on slot, read value, set `READ` bit; trigger `Block::destroy()` if needed

**Segment retirement (lines 105-127):**
- `Block::destroy()` sets `DESTROY` bit and checks if any slot still in use (`READ == 0`)
- If all slots read, deallocates block; otherwise, defers to thread that last reads a slot

---

**KEY ANCHORS:**

**crossbeam-deque/deque.rs:**
- `struct Inner` — line 114
- `struct Buffer` — line 29
- `Worker::push()` — line 399
- `Worker::pop()` — line 450
- `Stealer::steal()` — line 641

**crossbeam-queue/array_queue.rs:**
- `struct ArrayQueue` — line 52
- `struct Slot` — line 18
- `push()` — line 127 (core loop at line 203)
- `pop()` — line 318

**crossbeam-queue/seg_queue.rs:**
- `struct SegQueue` — line 161
- `struct Block` — line 56
- `struct Slot` — line 35
- `push()` — line 214
- `pop()` — line 364

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need to provide a comprehensive structural summary. Let me compile the findings:

## CROSSBEAM-CHANNEL STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS

### 1. **src/channel.rs** — Main API & Dispatch

**SenderFlavor enum** (line 371-380):
- `Array(counter::Sender<flavors::array::Channel>)` — bounded ring buffer
- `List(counter::Sender<flavors::list::Channel>)` — unbounded linked list  
- `Zero(counter::Sender<flavors::zero::Channel>)` — rendezvous (zero-capacity)

**ReceiverFlavor enum** (line 729-747):
- `Array`, `List`, `Zero` (same as sender)
- `At(Arc<flavors::at::Channel>)` — timer-based, delivers at instant
- `Tick(Arc<flavors::tick::Channel>)` — periodic timer
- `Never(flavors::never::Channel)` — never delivers

**Dispatch pattern**: `Sender::send`/`Sender::try_send` match on flavor (lines 410-416, 446-451), `Receiver::recv`/`Receiver::try_recv` match on flavor (lines 778-800, 831-856). Timeout variants: `send_timeout`/`send_deadline` (lines 495-547), `recv_timeout`/`recv_deadline` (lines 896-969).

**Public API functions**:
- `unbounded()` → (Sender, Receiver) at line 50
- `bounded(cap)` → (Sender, Receiver) at line 113
- `after(duration)` → Receiver<Instant> at line 181
- `at(when)` → Receiver<Instant> at line 232
- `tick(duration)` → Receiver<Instant> at line 335
- `never()` → Receiver<T> at line 275

**Trait impls on Sender/Receiver**:
- `Clone` (lines 686-696, 1199-1211)
- `Drop` (lines 674-684, 1184-1197) 
- `SelectHandle` (lines 1386-1446, 1448-1536) — enables select! participation
- `IntoIterator` for `&Receiver` and `Receiver` (lines 1220-1236)
- `Debug`, `Send`, `Sync`, `UnwindSafe`, `RefUnwindSafe`

---

### 2. **Flavors** — Channel Implementations

**Common `SelectHandle` trait** (select.rs:99-123):
- `try_select(&mut Token) -> bool` — attempt operation non-blocking
- `deadline() -> Option<Instant>` — return deadline if any
- `register(Operation, &Context) -> bool` — register for blocking
- `unregister(Operation)` — cleanup after blocking
- `accept(&mut Token, &Context) -> bool` — complete after wakeup
- `is_ready() -> bool` — check if operation won't block
- `watch/unwatch` — readiness notification

**flavors/array.rs** (lines 59-605):
- **Primitive**: Bounded MPMC ring buffer (Dmitry Vyukov's algorithm)
- **Capacity**: Fixed positive (set at creation)
- **Token**: `ArrayToken { slot: *const u8, stamp: usize }`
- **Key methods**: `start_send`, `write`, `start_recv`, `read`, `try_send`, `send`, `try_recv`, `recv`

**flavors/list.rs** (lines 170-708):
- **Primitive**: Unbounded linked list of blocks (each block holds 31 messages)
- **Capacity**: Unlimited
- **Token**: `ListToken { block: *const u8, offset: usize }`
- **Key methods**: same as array, plus `discard_all_messages`

**flavors/zero.rs** (lines 101-491):
- **Primitive**: Rendezvous channel (zero-capacity, synchronous handoff)
- **Capacity**: 0
- **Token**: `ZeroToken(*mut ())` — pointer to Packet
- **Packet**: Stack or heap-allocated message exchange buffer
- **Key methods**: sender/receiver pair up via `start_send`/`start_recv`, exchange through Packet

**flavors/at.rs** (lines 18-195):
- **Primitive**: Timer delivering single message at deadline
- **Token**: `AtToken = Option<Instant>`
- **Key methods**: `try_recv`, `recv`, `read` return `Instant`

**flavors/tick.rs** (lines 60-210):
- **Primitive**: Periodic timer delivering messages at intervals
- **Token**: `TickToken = Option<Instant>`
- **Key methods**: uses `AtomicCell<Align<Instant>>` for lock-free timestamp updates

**flavors/never.rs** (lines 18-113):
- **Primitive**: Never delivers (always empty/full)
- **Token**: `NeverToken = ()`
- **Key methods**: all operations return Empty/Timeout errors

---

### 3. **src/select.rs** — Select Algorithm

**Select struct** (lines 616-1173):
- Builder for dynamic operation lists (not macro-based)
- Stores `Vec<(&dyn SelectHandle, usize, usize)>` — handles with index and address

**Core concepts**:
- **`Token`** (line 24-32): Union of all flavor tokens, passed to start/accept methods
- **`Operation`** (line 35-52): Unique identifier from address (`hook(&mut T)`)
- **`Selected`** enum (lines 56-92): `Waiting`, `Aborted`, `Disconnected`, `Operation(Operation)`

**Algorithm flow** (`run_select` lines 176-324):
1. **Start**: Try `try_select` on all handles (non-blocking attempt)
2. **Register**: If none ready, call `register` on all handles to block
3. **Decide**: Wait via `Context::wait_until(deadline)` for wakeup
4. **Unregister**: Cleanup all registrations
5. **Accept**: Find selected handle, call `accept` to complete operation

**Select vs select_macro.rs**:
- `select.rs`: Dynamic `Select` struct for runtime operation lists
- `select_macro.rs`: Compile-time `select!` macro (not examined here) — generates static dispatch code

**Two execution modes**:
- `select`: Must complete operation (panics if dropped)
- `ready`: Only returns index, operation may be re-attempted

---

### 4. **src/waker.rs** — Thread Wakeup Mechanism

**Purpose**: Manages threads blocked on channel operations

**`Waker` struct** (lines 32-169):
- `selectors: Vec<Entry>` — threads waiting for specific operations
- `observers: Vec<Entry>` — threads waiting for readiness notifications

**`Entry` struct** (lines 17-26):
- `oper: Operation` — which operation
- `packet: *mut ()` — optional data packet (for zero flavor)
- `cx: Context` — thread context for wakeup

**Key methods**:
- `register/register_with_packet`: Add blocked thread
- `try_select`: Find different thread, wake it (lines 84-111)
- `notify`: Wake all observers (line 145)
- `disconnect`: Wake everyone with Disconnected (line 155)

**`SyncWaker`** (lines 182-278): Thread-safe wrapper with Mutex + empty-flag optimization

---

### 5. **src/context.rs** — Thread-Local Blocking Context

**Purpose**: Per-thread state for select/blocking operations

**`Context` struct** (lines 20-183):
- Thread-local cached instance (lines 48-70)
- `Arc<Inner>` shared between select and waker

**`Inner` fields**:
- `select: AtomicUsize` — current `Selected` state
- `packet: AtomicPtr<()>` — packet pointer from waker
- `thread: Thread` — handle for unpark
- `thread_id: ThreadId` — for self-prevention in waker

**Key methods**:
- `try_select(Selected)` — CAS from Waiting to new state (line 98)
- `selected()` — read current state (line 113)
- `wait_until(deadline)` — park thread until selected or timeout (line 144)
- `unpark()` — wake the thread (line 173)

---

### 6. **src/counter.rs** — Reference Counting

**Purpose**: Shared ownership of channel, triggers destruction on last drop

**`Counter<C>` struct** (lines 12-24):
- `senders: AtomicUsize` — sender count
- `receivers: AtomicUsize` — receiver count  
- `destroy: AtomicBool` — whether channel was deallocated
- `chan: C` — actual channel flavor

**`Sender`/`Receiver` wrappers** (lines 40-156):
- `acquire()` — increment count (abort if overflow, lines 51-64)
- `release(disconnect_fn)` — decrement; if last, call disconnect and possibly deallocate (lines 69-77, 128-136)
- Deallocate only when both sides dropped (destroy flag ensures single deallocation)

**Deref behavior**: Sender/Receiver deref to inner channel flavor

---

### 7. **src/err.rs** — Error Types

**Send errors**:
- `SendError<T>(T)` — channel disconnected (line 12)
- `TrySendError<T>` — `Full(T)` or `Disconnected(T)` (line 20)
- `SendTimeoutError<T>` — `Timeout(T)` or `Disconnected(T)` (line 37)

**Recv errors**:
- `RecvError` — empty & disconnected (line 54)
- `TryRecvError` — `Empty` or `Disconnected` (line 60)
- `RecvTimeoutError` — `Timeout` or `Disconnected` (line 75)

**Select errors**:
- `TrySelectError` — all would block (line 92)
- `SelectTimeoutError` — timeout before ready (line 100)
- `TryReadyError`, `ReadyTimeoutError` — for ready() operations

---

### 8. **Examples**

**fibonacci.rs** (line 8):
- Demonstrates `bounded(0)` rendezvous channel
- Spawns fibonacci generator thread, consumes first 20 values via iterator

**matching.rs** (line 53):
- Demonstrates `select!` macro for bidirectional communication
- Each thread sends or receives name on shared channel (matching problem)

**stopwatch.rs** (line 44):
- Demonstrates `tick()` for periodic timing + `select!` for multi-channel waiting
- Combines timer channel with SIGINT signal channel

---

**Key architectural insight**: crossbeam-channel uses a **flavor-based dispatch** where the public Sender/Receiver types are thin wrappers over flavor-specific implementations, all unified through the `SelectHandle` trait for select operations. Reference counting and waker/context enable thread-safe blocking and wakeup across all flavors.

content.text: 我现在已经完成了对全部 6 个 subcrate 的梳理。我已经掌握了所需的一切信息，可以设计一套涵盖初学者、中级和高级阶段的结构化课程大纲。现在输出大纲。

input.rationale: crossbeam 是一个 Rust 并发编程工具集 workspace，包含 6 个子 crate（channel/deque/epoch/queue/utils/skiplist），主 crate 仅做重导出。本大纲按「先会用、再懂原理、最后攻核心」的顺序设计 7 个单元、33 篇讲义。第 1 单元让读者从零认识项目定位、工作区结构与构建方式，并以最易上手的「作用域线程 scope」作为第一个动手实践；第 2 单元拆解 crossbeam-utils 的并发原语基石（Backoff/CachePadded/AtomicCell/Parker/WaitGroup/ShardedLock 等），它们是其它数据结构的公共依赖；第 3 单元进入最具规模的 crossbeam-channel，从基本使用到 flavors 架构、阻塞唤醒机制，再到 select 动态算法与 select! 宏；第 4 单元讲并发队列 ArrayQueue/SegQueue 的防 ABA 设计；第 5 单元攻克难度最高的 crossbeam-epoch（基于 epoch 的内存回收），它是 deque 与 skiplist 的安全基石，因此放在两者之前；第 6 单元基于 epoch 理解工作窃取双端队列（Chase-Lev）；第 7 单元以无锁跳表作为 epoch 综合应用，并以 loom/miri/tsan 测试与基准总结并发正确性与工程实践。依赖关系确保每个主题在所需前置知识（如 epoch 在 deque/skiplist 之前、SelectHandle 在各 flavor 之前、AtomicCell 在 queue 之前）之后展开。

units.title: 初识 crossbeam：定位、工作区与第一个并发程序

lectures.title: crossbeam 项目概览与定位
lectures.topic: 介绍 crossbeam 是什么、解决哪些并发编程问题、包含哪些子 crate，以及它对 no_std/alloc/std 的分级支持。
learning_goals: 理解 crossbeam 作为并发工具集的定位与适用场景
认识 6 个子 crate（channel/deque/epoch/queue/utils/skiplist）的职责划分
了解 no_std、alloc、std 三级特性支持
minimal_modules: 项目定位与并发工具分类（原子/数据结构/内存管理/同步/工具）
子 crate 划分与各自职责
MSRV、许可证与版本约定
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README，用一句话向同事说明 crossbeam 与 std::sync::mpsc 的差异，并列出主 crate 重导出的 5 个工具。在本地执行 cargo doc --open 浏览 crossbeam 的模块树。

lectures.title: 工作区、构建与特性系统
lectures.topic: 讲解 Cargo workspace 的组织、std/alloc/no_std 特性门控的层层传递、CI 流水线与本地构建测试方式。
learning_goals: 理解 workspace 成员与各 crate 之间的 path 依赖关系
掌握 std/alloc 特性如何在子 crate 间层层传递
学会本地构建、测试与运行示例

lectures.topic: 剖析 src/lib.rs 如何把多个子 crate 重导出为统一的 crossbeam 命名空间，并支持 no_std。
learning_goals: 理解主 crate 作为门面（facade）的重导出模式
掌握 #[cfg(feature)] 条件编译在 lib.rs 中的运用
看清 utils/atomic/channel/epoch/queue/sync 的对外路径
minimal_modules: #![no_std] 与 extern crate std
pub use 重导出与 #[doc(inline)]
跨 crate 公开类型检查（tests/subcrates.rs）
lectures.practice_task: 对照 src/lib.rs 画出 crossbeam 的模块树，标注每个公开项来自哪个子 crate。写一个最小 crate 依赖 crossbeam，分别通过 crossbeam::channel 与 crossbeam_channel 两种路径调用 unbounded。

lectures.title: 作用域线程 scope 快速上手
lectures.topic: 以最易上手的 crossbeam_utils::thread::scope 为入口，让读者第一次动手写并发代码，理解作用域线程借用栈上变量的能力。
learning_goals: 掌握 scope/spawn/ScopedJoinHandle 的基本用法
理解作用域线程如何安全地借用栈上 &T
学会处理 spawn 线程的 panic 与返回值
minimal_modules: scope 的签名与生命周期 'env
spawn 与 ScopedJoinHandle::join
作用域结束时自动等待所有线程
lectures.practice_task: 用 scope 启动 4 个线程并发对一段 &mut [i32] 切片求和（每个线程处理一段并写入局部累加变量），在作用域内收集结果，体现相比 std::thread::spawn 能借用栈上数据的优势。

lectures.title: Backoff 自旋退避
lectures.topic: 讲解 Backoff 在自旋锁/CAS 循环中的指数退避策略：spin 与 snooze 两个阶段，以及何时应转为阻塞。
learning_goals: 理解 spin/snooze 两阶段退避原理
掌握 is_completed 判断何时停止自旋
学会在 CAS 循环中正确使用 Backoff
minimal_modules: SPIN_LIMIT/YIELD_LIMIT 常量与指数增长
spin() 纯自旋 vs snooze() 自旋后 yield_now
is_completed() 与阻塞决策
lectures.practice_task: 实现一个简单自旋锁 SpinLock<T>，在 CAS 失败时用 Backoff::spin 退避；再实现一个用 snooze 的变体，写微基准比较两者在 4 线程高争用下的吞吐。

lectures.title: CachePadded 与伪共享
lectures.topic: 讲解 CachePadded 如何通过按缓存行对齐消除伪共享（false sharing），以及不同架构的对齐策略。
learning_goals: 理解伪共享的成因与危害
掌握 CachePadded 的使用方式与对齐值
学会判断哪些并发字段需要 padding
minimal_modules: 伪共享问题与缓存行概念
repr(C) 与架构相关 align（64/128/256/32）
Deref 实现与性能取舍
lectures.practice_task: 构造两个被多线程频繁原子更新的相邻 AtomicUsize，对比包装与不包装 CachePadded 的性能差异，用基准展示伪共享导致的性能下降。

lectures.title: AtomicCell 任意类型原子单元
lectures.topic: 剖析 AtomicCell<T> 如何对任意类型提供原子 load/store/swap/CAS：对可转原子类型直接原子操作，否则用全局序列锁数组兜底。
learning_goals: 理解 AtomicCell 的两条实现路径（直接原子 vs 序列锁）
掌握序列锁的乐观读/写协议
认识 MaybeUninit 与 unsafe 边界
minimal_modules: repr(transparent)+UnsafeCell+MaybeUninit 布局
can_transmute 判定与 atomic! 宏分发
全局 67 把 cache-padded 锁的 lock() 与乐观读校验
lectures.practice_task: 用 AtomicCell<u64> 与 AtomicCell<(u64,u64)> 分别做并发交换测试。阅读 atomic! 宏与 lock()，画出一组大尺寸 T 写操作时选择哪把锁。

lectures.title: AtomicConsume 与序列锁
lectures.topic: 讲解 AtomicConsume trait 提供的 consume 内存序及其在 ARM/AArch64 上的优化实现，以及窄指针宽度下的 wide 序列锁。
learning_goals: 理解 consume 内存序相对 acquire 的优势
掌握 AtomicConsume 在不同架构的实现差异
认识 seq_lock_wide 防止计数回绕的设计
minimal_modules: AtomicConsume trait 与 load_consume
ARM/AArch64 的 Relaxed + compiler_fence(Acquire) 优化
宽计数器 seq_lock_wide 的回绕防护
lectures.practice_task: 为一个自定义 AtomicPtr 包装实现 load_consume 读取，对比 Acquire 与 Consume 在依赖链上的语义差异。阅读 seq_lock_wide.rs 说明为何指针宽度≤32 需要 wide 版本。

lectures.title: Parker 线程停放原语
lectures.topic: 剖析 Parker/Unparker 的状态机（EMPTY/PARKED/NOTIFIED）与 park/unpark 的 condvar 实现，以及避免丢失唤醒的设计。
learning_goals: 理解 Parker 三态状态机与令牌语义
掌握 park/park_timeout 的阻塞逻辑
认识 unpark 的先发通知如何防止丢失唤醒
minimal_modules: Inner 三态与 Mutex+Condvar 组合
park()：检查通知→置 PARKED→等待→处理伪唤醒
unpark()：CAS 置 NOTIFIED 并 notify_one
lectures.practice_task: 用 Parker 实现一个简单事件等待器（线程 A park，线程 B 在条件成立后 unpark）。构造一个「先 unpark 后 park」的场景，验证不会永久阻塞。

lectures.title: WaitGroup 与 ShardedLock
lectures.topic: 讲解 WaitGroup（同步一组任务完成）与 ShardedLock（分片读写锁，读路径只锁一个分片）的设计与实现。
learning_goals: 掌握 WaitGroup 的引用计数 + condvar 等待机制
理解 ShardedLock 分片降低读争用的原理
认识线程局部注册表带来的稳定分片索引
minimal_modules: WaitGroup clone/drop/wait 的计数语义
ShardedLock 8 分片与 RwLock，写路径全锁
线程局部 REGISTRATION 的稳定分片索引
lectures.practice_task: 用 WaitGroup 协调 N 个工作线程全部完成后再汇总。再写一个读多写少场景，对比 std::sync::RwLock 与 ShardedLock 在 8 线程读吞吐上的差异。

lectures.title: 作用域线程实现内幕
lectures.topic: 深入 thread.rs，理解 scope 如何用 WaitGroup 协调、SharedVec 管理句柄，以及把 'env 闭包转 'static 的 unsafe 技巧与 panic 捕获。
learning_goals: 理解 Scope 内部 handle 列表与 WaitGroup 的协作
掌握闭包 'env→'static 的生命周期转换与安全保证
认识作用域结束时的 join 与 panic 收集
minimal_modules: Scope 结构与 SharedVec<JoinHandle>
spawn 的 Box<dyn FnOnce+'env> 转换与 AbortOnPanic
scope 结束：wait 嵌套作用域→join 全部线程→收集 panic
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 scope 实现，解释为何在 scope 返回前所有 spawn 的线程一定已结束（从而借用安全）。构造一个线程 panic 的场景，验证 scope 返回 Err 且不影响其它线程。

lectures.title: channel 总览与基本使用
lectures.topic: 介绍 crossbeam-channel 的三种基本通道（有界 bounded、无界 unbounded、零容量 rendezvous），以及 send/recv/迭代等基础用法。
learning_goals: 掌握 bounded/unbounded/bounded(0) 三类通道的语义
理解 send/recv 的阻塞与 TryRecv/Send 错误
学会用 Receiver 作为迭代器消费消息
minimal_modules: unbounded()/bounded()/bounded(0) 的区别
Sender/Receiver 公共方法与错误类型概览
示例：fibonacci 生成器用 bounded(0) 会合
lectures.practice_task: 实现一个生产者-消费者：一个线程用 unbounded 发送 1..100，主线程用 for 循环消费并求和。再改用 bounded(8) 观察背压，思考何时应选 bounded。

lectures.title: counter 引用计数与 err 错误类型
lectures.topic: 剖析 counter.rs 如何用 AtomicUsize 计数管理多 Sender/Receiver 并在最后引用释放时销毁通道，以及 err.rs 的错误类型体系。
learning_goals: 理解 Sender/Receiver 共享 Counter 的引用计数模型
掌握 acquire/release 与 destroy 标志的销毁协议
认识 send/recv/select 各类错误枚举
minimal_modules: Counter：senders/receivers/destroy/chan 字段
acquire/release/deallocate 协议与唯一销毁
err.rs：TrySend/Send/Recv/Timeout/Select 错误家族
lectures.practice_task: 克隆多个 Sender 与 Receiver，在不同线程中 drop，追踪 senders/receivers 何时归零并触发销毁。编写一个表格总结 6 类错误分别在什么条件下产生。

lectures.title: flavors 架构与 SelectHandle trait
lectures.topic: 讲解 channel 的 flavor 派发架构：SenderFlavor/ReceiverFlavor 枚举如何把操作路由到具体 flavor，以及统一的 SelectHandle trait。
learning_goals: 理解 SenderFlavor/ReceiverFlavor 枚举派发模式
掌握 SelectHandle trait 的统一接口（try_select/register/accept 等）
认识 Token 作为各 flavor 操作状态的载体
minimal_modules: SenderFlavor/ReceiverFlavor 枚举与 match 派发
SelectHandle trait 的核心方法语义
Token 联合体与 Operation/Selected
lectures.practice_task: 画一张表，列出 array/list/zero/at/tick/never 六种 flavor 各自实现 SelectHandle 的哪些关键行为。在 channel.rs 中找到 send/recv 的 match 分支并标注每条分支对应的 flavor 文件。

lectures.title: array flavor：有界环形缓冲
lectures.topic: 剖析 flavors/array.rs 的有界 MPMC 环形缓冲实现（Vyukov 算法）：槽位 stamp、start_send/write、start_recv/read 与阻塞协作。
learning_goals: 理解 stamp 标记的可写/可读槽位协议
掌握 send/recv 的 start→commit 两阶段与 Token 保存
认识有界缓冲与 Waker 的阻塞配合
minimal_modules: ArrayToken{slot,stamp} 与环形槽布局
start_send/start_recv 占位 + write/read 提交
与 Waker 协作实现阻塞 send/recv
lectures.practice_task: 对照 array.rs 画出一次成功 send+recv 的槽位 stamp 变化时序。构造容量为 2 的通道，2 个发送者各发 5 条、1 个接收者消费，用日志验证 stamp 状态机。

lectures.title: list flavor：无界链表
lectures.topic: 剖析 flavors/list.rs 的无界链表实现：每块 31 条消息的分块链表、按需扩容，以及最后一个 receiver drop 时丢弃全部消息。
learning_goals: 理解分块链表（每块 31 消息）的存储结构
掌握 ListToken{block,offset} 的定位与扩容
认识 list 作为 unbounded 通道默认实现及销毁语义
minimal_modules: Block 分块与尾指针扩容
start_send/write 按 offset 写入与 ListToken
discard_all_messages 在最后 receiver 离开时清理
lectures.practice_task: 用 unbounded 通道连续发送 1000 条消息但不接收，阅读 list.rs 估算分配了多少个 block（每块 31 条）。说明为何 list 不需要像 array 那样处理容量上限。

lectures.title: zero flavor：零容量会合通道
lectures.topic: 剖析 flavors/zero.rs 的 rendezvous（零容量）通道：send 与 recv 必须同时在场配对，通过 Packet 直接交接所有权。
learning_goals: 理解零容量通道的会合（rendezvous）语义
掌握 Packet 作为交接缓冲与 ZeroToken
认识 start_send/start_recv 配对与唤醒协议
minimal_modules: 零容量=无缓冲，send/recv 必须配对
Packet 交接与 ZeroToken 指针
配对成功后的相互唤醒
lectures.practice_task: 用 bounded(0) 实现一个严格同步的握手：线程 A 发送后必须等 B 接收才能继续。阅读 zero.rs 解释为何发送者不能先把消息放进缓冲。

lectures.title: Context 与 Waker：阻塞与唤醒机制
lectures.topic: 剖析 context.rs（线程局部阻塞上下文 + try_select/wait_until/unpark）与 waker.rs（管理被阻塞线程的 selectors/observers 队列）。
learning_goals: 理解 Context 的线程局部缓存与 Inner 的 CAS 选择
掌握 Waker/SyncWaker 的注册/选择/通知/断开
认识 select 算法与阻塞唤醒的衔接
minimal_modules: Context/Inner：select 状态 + packet + 线程句柄
try_select CAS + wait_until 阻塞 + unpark
Waker selectors/observers 与 SyncWaker 互斥
lectures.practice_task: 用一个有界(1) 通道制造阻塞 recv：发送者延迟 1 秒发送，接收者阻塞。阅读 context.rs/wait_until 与 waker.rs，画出从「接收者 park」到「发送者唤醒接收者」的完整调用链。

lectures.title: tick/at/never：时间与特殊 flavor
lectures.topic: 讲解 at（在指定时刻投递一次）、tick（周期性投递）、never（永不投递）三种特殊 Receiver 的实现，以及它们在 select 中的作用。
learning_goals: 理解 at/tick 作为只读 Receiver 的定时语义
掌握 never 作为选择失败兜底的用途
认识这些 flavor 如何与 select 组合实现超时/周期任务
minimal_modules: at：单次定时投递 Instant
tick：周期定时与 AtomicCell 时间戳
never：始终 Empty 的兜底通道
lectures.practice_task: 用 tick(Duration::from_secs(1)) 实现每秒打印一次的定时器；用 after(Duration::from_secs(5)) 配合 select 给一个长任务加超时。说明 never 在 select 中何时有用。

lectures.title: select 动态选择算法
lectures.topic: 剖析 select.rs 的 Select 动态多路选择：start（try_select 全部）→register（注册阻塞）→decide（等待唤醒）→unregister→accept（完成）的五阶段算法。
learning_goals: 理解 Select 构建操作列表并动态多路选择
掌握五阶段选择算法与公平性保证
认识 select（必须完成）与 ready（仅返回就绪索引）两种模式
minimal_modules: Select 构建 &dyn SelectHandle 列表
run_select：try_select→register→wait_until→unregister→accept
select vs ready，Selected 状态机
lectures.practice_task: 用 Select 在两个 unbounded 通道间做动态选择，打印先到的消息。再实现一个超时版本：加入一个 after(...) 通道作为第三路。对照 select.rs 说明每个操作如何被 register 与 accept。

lectures.title: select! 宏
lectures.topic: 剖析 select_macro.rs 的 select! 宏：解析阶段（@list/@case）与代码生成阶段（@init/@count/@add/@complete），以及编译期优化与容量限制。
learning_goals: 理解声明式宏的分阶段解析与代码生成
掌握 select! 展开后的运行时逻辑与 select.rs 的关系
认识 default 分支与 case 数量上限
minimal_modules: 解析阶段：@list/@list_error/@case 与语法诊断
代码生成：@init 优化/@count/@add/@complete
select! 与底层 Select 的对应
lectures.practice_task: 写一段 select! 同时 recv 两个通道并带 default 超时分支，用 cargo expand（或手动推理）查看展开结果，标注展开代码对应 select.rs 的哪几个阶段。

lectures.title: ArrayQueue：有界 MPMC 队列
lectures.topic: 剖析 array_queue.rs 的有界 MPMC 环形队列：用 lap（幂次）+ index 编码与 slot stamp 防止 ABA，head/tail 的 CAS 推进。
learning_goals: 理解 lap/index 编码与 one_lap 防 ABA 机制
掌握 Slot 的 stamp 状态机（可写/可读）
认识 CachePadded 的 head/tail 避免伪共享
minimal_modules: Slot{stamp,value} 与 lap 编码
push：CAS tail + 写值 + 更新 stamp
pop：CAS head + 读值 + 重置 stamp 为下一圈
lectures.practice_task: 用 ArrayQueue 在 4 生产者 4 消费者下传递消息并验证无丢失。阅读 array_queue.rs 解释：若不用 lap（代次）会出现什么 ABA 问题。

lectures.title: SegQueue：无界分段队列
lectures.topic: 剖析 seg_queue.rs 的无界分段队列：链表式 Block（每块 31 槽）、index 编码带 HAS_NEXT 位、按需分配新块与块回收。
learning_goals: 理解 Block 链表与 BLOCK_CAP=31 的设计
掌握 index 编码（SHIFT/HAS_NEXT/LAP）与 slot 的 WRITE/READ/DESTROY 位
认识段的分配、推进与回收协议
minimal_modules: Block/Slot 布局与状态位
push：预分配下一段 + CAS tail + 写值置 WRITE
pop：CAS head + 等待 WRITE + 读值置 READ + 段回收
lectures.practice_task: 用 SegQueue 做无界传递并持续发送 1e6 条消息。阅读 seg_queue.rs 估算段分配次数，并解释最后一个读取某槽的线程负责释放段的协议。

units.title: crossbeam-epoch：基于 epoch 的内存回收

lectures.title: epoch 内存回收：问题背景与 API
lectures.topic: 引入并发数据结构的内存回收难题，讲解基于 epoch 的回收思想，并概览 crossbeam-epoch 的公共 API（pin/Guard/Collector/Atomic/Shared/Owned）。
learning_goals: 理解无锁数据结构删除节点的安全回收难题
掌握 epoch 回收的基本思想（延迟两个 epoch）
认识 crossbeam-epoch 的核心类型与 pin 入口
minimal_modules: 问题背景：删除与并发读取的竞争
epoch 回收原理：标记→延迟→销毁
公共 API：pin/default_collector/is_pinned 与重导出
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 lib.rs 文档注释，用一段话说明「为什么 GC 语言里无锁结构更简单」。在本地执行 cargo doc -p crossbeam-epoch 浏览 pin/Guard/Atomic 的文档。

lectures.title: Atomic/Shared/Owned 原子指针与标签
lectures.topic: 剖析 atomic.rs：Atomic<T> 原子指针、低地址位标签打包、Owned（独占）与 Shared<'g,T>（借用）的区别，以及 load/store/swap/compare_exchange。
learning_goals: 理解 AtomicPtr + 低地址位标签打包
掌握 Owned vs Shared 的所有权语义与 Pointer trait
认识 compare_exchange 同时比较指针与标签
minimal_modules: Atomic<T> 与 low_bits/compose_tag/decompose_tag
Owned/Shared/Pointer 的关系与转换
load/load_consume/store/swap/compare_exchange
lectures.practice_task: 用 Atomic<T> 存一个带 2 位标签的指针，写一段代码用 compare_exchange 原子地更新指针并修改标签。说明 Shared 的生命周期 'g 为何与 Guard 绑定。

lectures.title: Guard 与 pin 机制
lectures.topic: 剖析 guard.rs 的 RAII Guard：pin 增加保护计数、defer/defer_destroy/defer_unchecked 延迟销毁，以及 deferred.rs 的类型擦除闭包与 default.rs 的全局收集器。
learning_goals: 理解 Guard 的 RAII 与可重入 guard_count
掌握 defer 系列方法的延迟执行语义
认识 Deferred 类型擦除闭包与全局 pin 入口
minimal_modules: Guard RAII + guard_count 可重入 pin
defer/defer_destroy/defer_unchecked
Deferred 内联/装箱闭包与全局 pin()
lectures.practice_task: 用 pin() 获得 Guard，defer_destroy 一段共享数据，观察它在后续 pin 周期后才真正释放（用计数/日志验证）。说明 defer_unchecked 为何是 unsafe。

lectures.title: Collector 与参与者注册
lectures.topic: 剖析 collector.rs 的 Collector（Arc<Global>）与 LocalHandle：参与者注册到全局链表、LocalHandle::pin 的可重入保护与周期性回收。
learning_goals: 理解 Collector 与 LocalHandle 的关系
掌握参与者注册到全局链表的协议
认识 pin 时记录局部 epoch 与 SeqCst fence 的作用
minimal_modules: Collector{Arc<Global>} 与 LocalHandle
register：插入全局参与者链表
LocalHandle::pin：guard_count++/记录 epoch/fence/周期 collect
lectures.practice_task: 创建一个自定义 Collector，register 两个参与者并各自 pin，用日志观察 LocalHandle 的 guard_count 变化。说明为什么需要 fence 保证回收安全。

lectures.title: internal：全局状态、epoch 推进与垃圾回收
lectures.topic: 剖析 internal.rs 的 Global（参与者链表+全局垃圾队列+epoch）与 Local（线程局部 garbage bag），以及 try_advance 推进 epoch、collect 销毁过期垃圾的完整协议。
learning_goals: 理解 Global/Local 的字段布局与协作
掌握 try_advance 的「所有参与者已离开旧 epoch」判定
认识 Bag/SealedBag 的两 epoch 延迟销毁原理
minimal_modules: Global：locals 链表 + queue 垃圾队列 + epoch
try_advance：fence + 检查所有参与者 + 推进
Bag/SealedBag 与 epoch 差≥2 才销毁
lectures.practice_task: 画一张 epoch 推进时序图：参与者 A 在 epoch=0 pin→defer 垃圾→B 推进 epoch→何时该垃圾被销毁。解释为何要等 2 个 epoch 而非 1 个。

lectures.title: 工作窃取模型与公共 API
lectures.topic: 讲解工作窃取调度模型：Injector（全局 FIFO）、Worker（FIFO/LIFO 本地队列）、Stealer（可共享窃取句柄）与 Steal 枚举的 API 与使用范式。
learning_goals: 理解 Injector/Worker/Stealer/Steal 的角色与典型调度结构
掌握 push/pop/steal/steal_batch_and_pop 的用法
认识 Steal::Retry 需要重试的语义
minimal_modules: Worker::new_fifo/new_lifo、stealer()、Injector
steal/steal_batch/steal_batch_and_pop
Steal{Empty,Success,Retry} 与重试模式
lectures.practice_task: 搭建一个迷你工作窃取调度器：1 个 Injector + N 个 Worker，主线程往 Injector 注入任务，工作线程先取本地、再偷同伴、再取 Injector。验证任务全部被执行。

lectures.title: Chase-Lev 双端队列实现
lectures.topic: 深入 deque.rs 的 Chase-Lev 实现：front/back 原子索引、幂次环形 Buffer、FIFO/LIFO 的 push/pop 方向差异、steal 协议与 buffer 扩缩容及 epoch 回收。
learning_goals: 理解 Chase-Lev 的 front/back 索引与环形缓冲
掌握 FIFO vs LIFO 的 pop 方向差异与 steal 协议
认识 buffer resize 与用 epoch 安全回收旧缓冲
minimal_modules: Inner{front,back,buffer} + Buffer 环形与 CachePadded
push（写 back）/pop（FIFO 增 front / LIFO 减 back）/steal（CAS front）
resize 扩缩容与 epoch 延迟回收旧 buffer
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 deque.rs 的 steal()，解释为何 steal 返回 Retry 时应重试而非当作空。构造一个竞争场景：多个 Stealer 同时偷同一个 Worker，观察 Retry/Success/Empty 的分布。

units.title: crossbeam-skiplist 与综合：测试与正确性

lectures.title: 无锁跳表结构
lectures.topic: 剖析 base.rs 的无锁跳表节点结构：refs_and_height 打包引用计数与高度、Tower 多层指针、xorshift 高度生成与 mark_tower 删除标记。
learning_goals: 理解 Node 的 refs_and_height 打包与 Tower 布局
掌握高度生成与 max_height 提示
认识 mark_tower 自顶向下的逻辑删除标记
minimal_modules: Node{value,key,refs_and_height,tower} 布局
xorshift 高度生成与 max_height 提示
引用计数 try_increment/decrement/finalize 与 mark_tower
lectures.practice_task: 画出一个 3 层、4 个节点的跳表示意图，标注每个节点的 tower 指针。阅读 base.rs 说明 mark_tower 为何要从最高层向第 0 层逐层标记。

lectures.title: 跳表操作与 epoch 集成
lectures.topic: 讲解跳表的并发插入/查找/删除（search_bound、协助摘除已删除节点），以及如何用 crossbeam-epoch 的 Guard 延迟释放节点；并介绍 SkipMap/SkipSet 包装。
learning_goals: 理解 search_bound 的逐层下降与协助清理
掌握并发 insert/get/remove 的无锁协调
认识 epoch Guard 在节点释放中的关键作用与 SkipMap/SkipSet 接口

并发 insert/remove 与引用计数收尾
epoch guard.defer_unchecked 释放节点 + Map/Set 包装
lectures.practice_task: 用 SkipMap 在多线程并发 insert/get/remove，验证最终状态一致。阅读 base.rs 找到所有 defer_unchecked 调用点，说明每处释放的对象与触发时机。

lectures.title: 测试、loom 与并发正确性
lectures.topic: 总结 crossbeam 如何保证并发正确性：cargo test、miri（UB 检测）、tsan/san（数据竞争与 sanitizer）、loom（模型检查）与 CI 流水线及基准对比。
learning_goals: 理解 loom 状态空间模型检查的原理
掌握 miri/tsan/san 在并发代码中的作用
认识 CI 流水线与 benchmarks 的工程意义
minimal_modules: loom：crossbeam_loom cfg 与内部 primitive 抽象
miri/san/tsan：UB 与数据竞争检测
CI 流水线与 benchmarks 性能对比
lectures.practice_task: 在 crossbeam-epoch 上运行 ci/crossbeam-epoch-loom.sh（或 cargo +nightly miri test -p crossbeam-utils）观察输出。阅读 benchmarks 的 run.sh，说明 crossbeam 与其它通道实现的对比维度。

data.head: 6195355ef1862f2c6172365d00645cb6f77417dc
data.rationale: crossbeam 是一个 Rust 并发编程工具集 workspace，包含 6 个子 crate（channel/deque/epoch/queue/utils/skiplist），主 crate 仅做重导出。本大纲按「先会用、再懂原理、最后攻核心」的顺序设计 7 个单元、33 篇讲义。第 1 单元让读者从零认识项目定位、工作区结构与构建方式，并以最易上手的「作用域线程 scope」作为第一个动手实践；第 2 单元拆解 crossbeam-utils 的并发原语基石（Backoff/CachePadded/AtomicCell/Parker/WaitGroup/ShardedLock 等），它们是其它数据结构的公共依赖；第 3 单元进入最具规模的 crossbeam-channel，从基本使用到 flavors 架构、阻塞唤醒机制，再到 select 动态算法与 select! 宏；第 4 单元讲并发队列 ArrayQueue/SegQueue 的防 ABA 设计；第 5 单元攻克难度最高的 crossbeam-epoch（基于 epoch 的内存回收），它是 deque 与 skiplist 的安全基石，因此放在两者之前；第 6 单元基于 epoch 理解工作窃取双端队列（Chase-Lev）；第 7 单元以无锁跳表作为 epoch 综合应用，并以 loom/miri/tsan 测试与基准总结并发正确性与工程实践。依赖关系确保每个主题在所需前置知识（如 epoch 在 deque/skiplist 之前、SelectHandle 在各 flavor 之前、AtomicCell 在 queue 之前）之后展开。

units.title: 初识 crossbeam：定位、工作区与第一个并发程序

lectures.title: crossbeam 项目概览与定位
lectures.topic: 介绍 crossbeam 是什么、解决哪些并发编程问题、包含哪些子 crate，以及它对 no_std/alloc/std 的分级支持。
learning_goals: 理解 crossbeam 作为并发工具集的定位与适用场景
认识 6 个子 crate（channel/deque/epoch/queue/utils/skiplist）的职责划分
了解 no_std、alloc、std 三级特性支持
minimal_modules: 项目定位与并发工具分类（原子/数据结构/内存管理/同步/工具）
子 crate 划分与各自职责
MSRV、许可证与版本约定
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 README，用一句话向同事说明 crossbeam 与 std::sync::mpsc 的差异，并列出主 crate 重导出的 5 个工具。在本地执行 cargo doc --open 浏览 crossbeam 的模块树。

lectures.title: 工作区、构建与特性系统
lectures.topic: 讲解 Cargo workspace 的组织、std/alloc/no_std 特性门控的层层传递、CI 流水线与本地构建测试方式。
learning_goals: 理解 workspace 成员与各 crate 之间的 path 依赖关系
掌握 std/alloc 特性如何在子 crate 间层层传递
学会本地构建、测试与运行示例
minimal_modules: workspace members 与 path 依赖
特性门控：default=std、std 依赖 alloc、可选子 crate
CI 任务与本地构建/测试命令
lectures.practice_task: 克隆仓库后执行 cargo build --workspace 与 cargo test -p crossbeam-utils。再尝试 cargo build -p crossbeam --no-default-features --features alloc，观察哪些 API 仍然可用。

lectures.title: 主 crate 重导出门面与目录结构
lectures.topic: 剖析 src/lib.rs 如何把多个子 crate 重导出为统一的 crossbeam 命名空间，并支持 no_std。
learning_goals: 理解主 crate 作为门面（facade）的重导出模式
掌握 #[cfg(feature)] 条件编译在 lib.rs 中的运用
看清 utils/atomic/channel/epoch/queue/sync 的对外路径
minimal_modules: #![no_std] 与 extern crate std
pub use 重导出与 #[doc(inline)]
跨 crate 公开类型检查（tests/subcrates.rs）
lectures.practice_task: 对照 src/lib.rs 画出 crossbeam 的模块树，标注每个公开项来自哪个子 crate。写一个最小 crate 依赖 crossbeam，分别通过 crossbeam::channel 与 crossbeam_channel 两种路径调用 unbounded。

lectures.title: 作用域线程 scope 快速上手
lectures.topic: 以最易上手的 crossbeam_utils::thread::scope 为入口，让读者第一次动手写并发代码，理解作用域线程借用栈上变量的能力。
learning_goals: 掌握 scope/spawn/ScopedJoinHandle 的基本用法
理解作用域线程如何安全地借用栈上 &T
学会处理 spawn 线程的 panic 与返回值
minimal_modules: scope 的签名与生命周期 'env
spawn 与 ScopedJoinHandle::join
作用域结束时自动等待所有线程
lectures.practice_task: 用 scope 启动 4 个线程并发对一段 &mut [i32] 切片求和（每个线程处理一段并写入局部累加变量），在作用域内收集结果，体现相比 std::thread::spawn 能借用栈上数据的优势。

lectures.title: Backoff 自旋退避
lectures.topic: 讲解 Backoff 在自旋锁/CAS 循环中的指数退避策略：spin 与 snooze 两个阶段，以及何时应转为阻塞。
learning_goals: 理解 spin/snooze 两阶段退避原理
掌握 is_completed 判断何时停止自旋
学会在 CAS 循环中正确使用 Backoff
minimal_modules: SPIN_LIMIT/YIELD_LIMIT 常量与指数增长
spin() 纯自旋 vs snooze() 自旋后 yield_now
is_completed() 与阻塞决策
lectures.practice_task: 实现一个简单自旋锁 SpinLock<T>，在 CAS 失败时用 Backoff::spin 退避；再实现一个用 snooze 的变体，写微基准比较两者在 4 线程高争用下的吞吐。

lectures.title: CachePadded 与伪共享
lectures.topic: 讲解 CachePadded 如何通过按缓存行对齐消除伪共享（false sharing），以及不同架构的对齐策略。
learning_goals: 理解伪共享的成因与危害
掌握 CachePadded 的使用方式与对齐值
学会判断哪些并发字段需要 padding
minimal_modules: 伪共享问题与缓存行概念
repr(C) 与架构相关 align（64/128/256/32）
Deref 实现与性能取舍
lectures.practice_task: 构造两个被多线程频繁原子更新的相邻 AtomicUsize，对比包装与不包装 CachePadded 的性能差异，用基准展示伪共享导致的性能下降。

lectures.title: AtomicCell 任意类型原子单元
lectures.topic: 剖析 AtomicCell<T> 如何对任意类型提供原子 load/store/swap/CAS：对可转原子类型直接原子操作，否则用全局序列锁数组兜底。
learning_goals: 理解 AtomicCell 的两条实现路径（直接原子 vs 序列锁）
掌握序列锁的乐观读/写协议
认识 MaybeUninit 与 unsafe 边界
minimal_modules: repr(transparent)+UnsafeCell+MaybeUninit 布局
can_transmute 判定与 atomic! 宏分发
全局 67 把 cache-padded 锁的 lock() 与乐观读校验
lectures.practice_task: 用 AtomicCell<u64> 与 AtomicCell<(u64,u64)> 分别做并发交换测试。阅读 atomic! 宏与 lock()，画出一组大尺寸 T 写操作时选择哪把锁。

lectures.title: AtomicConsume 与序列锁
lectures.topic: 讲解 AtomicConsume trait 提供的 consume 内存序及其在 ARM/AArch64 上的优化实现，以及窄指针宽度下的 wide 序列锁。
learning_goals: 理解 consume 内存序相对 acquire 的优势
掌握 AtomicConsume 在不同架构的实现差异
认识 seq_lock_wide 防止计数回绕的设计
minimal_modules: AtomicConsume trait 与 load_consume
ARM/AArch64 的 Relaxed + compiler_fence(Acquire) 优化
宽计数器 seq_lock_wide 的回绕防护
lectures.practice_task: 为一个自定义 AtomicPtr 包装实现 load_consume 读取，对比 Acquire 与 Consume 在依赖链上的语义差异。阅读 seq_lock_wide.rs 说明为何指针宽度≤32 需要 wide 版本。

lectures.title: Parker 线程停放原语
lectures.topic: 剖析 Parker/Unparker 的状态机（EMPTY/PARKED/NOTIFIED）与 park/unpark 的 condvar 实现，以及避免丢失唤醒的设计。
learning_goals: 理解 Parker 三态状态机与令牌语义
掌握 park/park_timeout 的阻塞逻辑
认识 unpark 的先发通知如何防止丢失唤醒
minimal_modules: Inner 三态与 Mutex+Condvar 组合
park()：检查通知→置 PARKED→等待→处理伪唤醒
unpark()：CAS 置 NOTIFIED 并 notify_one
lectures.practice_task: 用 Parker 实现一个简单事件等待器（线程 A park，线程 B 在条件成立后 unpark）。构造一个「先 unpark 后 park」的场景，验证不会永久阻塞。

lectures.title: WaitGroup 与 ShardedLock
lectures.topic: 讲解 WaitGroup（同步一组任务完成）与 ShardedLock（分片读写锁，读路径只锁一个分片）的设计与实现。
learning_goals: 掌握 WaitGroup 的引用计数 + condvar 等待机制
理解 ShardedLock 分片降低读争用的原理
认识线程局部注册表带来的稳定分片索引
minimal_modules: WaitGroup clone/drop/wait 的计数语义
ShardedLock 8 分片与 RwLock，写路径全锁
线程局部 REGISTRATION 的稳定分片索引
lectures.practice_task: 用 WaitGroup 协调 N 个工作线程全部完成后再汇总。再写一个读多写少场景，对比 std::sync::RwLock 与 ShardedLock 在 8 线程读吞吐上的差异。

lectures.title: 作用域线程实现内幕
lectures.topic: 深入 thread.rs，理解 scope 如何用 WaitGroup 协调、SharedVec 管理句柄，以及把 'env 闭包转 'static 的 unsafe 技巧与 panic 捕获。
learning_goals: 理解 Scope 内部 handle 列表与 WaitGroup 的协作
掌握闭包 'env→'static 的生命周期转换与安全保证
认识作用域结束时的 join 与 panic 收集
minimal_modules: Scope 结构与 SharedVec<JoinHandle>
spawn 的 Box<dyn FnOnce+'env> 转换与 AbortOnPanic
scope 结束：wait 嵌套作用域→join 全部线程→收集 panic
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 scope 实现，解释为何在 scope 返回前所有 spawn 的线程一定已结束（从而借用安全）。构造一个线程 panic 的场景，验证 scope 返回 Err 且不影响其它线程。

lectures.title: channel 总览与基本使用
lectures.topic: 介绍 crossbeam-channel 的三种基本通道（有界 bounded、无界 unbounded、零容量 rendezvous），以及 send/recv/迭代等基础用法。
learning_goals: 掌握 bounded/unbounded/bounded(0) 三类通道的语义
理解 send/recv 的阻塞与 TryRecv/Send 错误
学会用 Receiver 作为迭代器消费消息
minimal_modules: unbounded()/bounded()/bounded(0) 的区别
Sender/Receiver 公共方法与错误类型概览
示例：fibonacci 生成器用 bounded(0) 会合
lectures.practice_task: 实现一个生产者-消费者：一个线程用 unbounded 发送 1..100，主线程用 for 循环消费并求和。再改用 bounded(8) 观察背压，思考何时应选 bounded。

lectures.title: counter 引用计数与 err 错误类型
lectures.topic: 剖析 counter.rs 如何用 AtomicUsize 计数管理多 Sender/Receiver 并在最后引用释放时销毁通道，以及 err.rs 的错误类型体系。
learning_goals: 理解 Sender/Receiver 共享 Counter 的引用计数模型
掌握 acquire/release 与 destroy 标志的销毁协议
认识 send/recv/select 各类错误枚举
minimal_modules: Counter：senders/receivers/destroy/chan 字段
acquire/release/deallocate 协议与唯一销毁
err.rs：TrySend/Send/Recv/Timeout/Select 错误家族
lectures.practice_task: 克隆多个 Sender 与 Receiver，在不同线程中 drop，追踪 senders/receivers 何时归零并触发销毁。编写一个表格总结 6 类错误分别在什么条件下产生。

lectures.title: flavors 架构与 SelectHandle trait
lectures.topic: 讲解 channel 的 flavor 派发架构：SenderFlavor/ReceiverFlavor 枚举如何把操作路由到具体 flavor，以及统一的 SelectHandle trait。
learning_goals: 理解 SenderFlavor/ReceiverFlavor 枚举派发模式
掌握 SelectHandle trait 的统一接口（try_select/register/accept 等）
认识 Token 作为各 flavor 操作状态的载体
minimal_modules: SenderFlavor/ReceiverFlavor 枚举与 match 派发
SelectHandle trait 的核心方法语义
Token 联合体与 Operation/Selected
lectures.practice_task: 画一张表，列出 array/list/zero/at/tick/never 六种 flavor 各自实现 SelectHandle 的哪些关键行为。在 channel.rs 中找到 send/recv 的 match 分支并标注每条分支对应的 flavor 文件。

lectures.title: array flavor：有界环形缓冲
lectures.topic: 剖析 flavors/array.rs 的有界 MPMC 环形缓冲实现（Vyukov 算法）：槽位 stamp、start_send/write、start_recv/read 与阻塞协作。
learning_goals: 理解 stamp 标记的可写/可读槽位协议
掌握 send/recv 的 start→commit 两阶段与 Token 保存
认识有界缓冲与 Waker 的阻塞配合
minimal_modules: ArrayToken{slot,stamp} 与环形槽布局
start_send/start_recv 占位 + write/read 提交
与 Waker 协作实现阻塞 send/recv
lectures.practice_task: 对照 array.rs 画出一次成功 send+recv 的槽位 stamp 变化时序。构造容量为 2 的通道，2 个发送者各发 5 条、1 个接收者消费，用日志验证 stamp 状态机。

lectures.title: list flavor：无界链表
lectures.topic: 剖析 flavors/list.rs 的无界链表实现：每块 31 条消息的分块链表、按需扩容，以及最后一个 receiver drop 时丢弃全部消息。
learning_goals: 理解分块链表（每块 31 消息）的存储结构
掌握 ListToken{block,offset} 的定位与扩容
认识 list 作为 unbounded 通道默认实现及销毁语义
minimal_modules: Block 分块与尾指针扩容
start_send/write 按 offset 写入与 ListToken
discard_all_messages 在最后 receiver 离开时清理
lectures.practice_task: 用 unbounded 通道连续发送 1000 条消息但不接收，阅读 list.rs 估算分配了多少个 block（每块 31 条）。说明为何 list 不需要像 array 那样处理容量上限。

lectures.title: zero flavor：零容量会合通道
lectures.topic: 剖析 flavors/zero.rs 的 rendezvous（零容量）通道：send 与 recv 必须同时在场配对，通过 Packet 直接交接所有权。
learning_goals: 理解零容量通道的会合（rendezvous）语义
掌握 Packet 作为交接缓冲与 ZeroToken
认识 start_send/start_recv 配对与唤醒协议
minimal_modules: 零容量=无缓冲，send/recv 必须配对
Packet 交接与 ZeroToken 指针
配对成功后的相互唤醒
lectures.practice_task: 用 bounded(0) 实现一个严格同步的握手：线程 A 发送后必须等 B 接收才能继续。阅读 zero.rs 解释为何发送者不能先把消息放进缓冲。

lectures.title: Context 与 Waker：阻塞与唤醒机制
lectures.topic: 剖析 context.rs（线程局部阻塞上下文 + try_select/wait_until/unpark）与 waker.rs（管理被阻塞线程的 selectors/observers 队列）。
learning_goals: 理解 Context 的线程局部缓存与 Inner 的 CAS 选择
掌握 Waker/SyncWaker 的注册/选择/通知/断开
认识 select 算法与阻塞唤醒的衔接
minimal_modules: Context/Inner：select 状态 + packet + 线程句柄
try_select CAS + wait_until 阻塞 + unpark
Waker selectors/observers 与 SyncWaker 互斥
lectures.practice_task: 用一个有界(1) 通道制造阻塞 recv：发送者延迟 1 秒发送，接收者阻塞。阅读 context.rs/wait_until 与 waker.rs，画出从「接收者 park」到「发送者唤醒接收者」的完整调用链。

lectures.title: tick/at/never：时间与特殊 flavor
lectures.topic: 讲解 at（在指定时刻投递一次）、tick（周期性投递）、never（永不投递）三种特殊 Receiver 的实现，以及它们在 select 中的作用。
learning_goals: 理解 at/tick 作为只读 Receiver 的定时语义
掌握 never 作为选择失败兜底的用途
认识这些 flavor 如何与 select 组合实现超时/周期任务
minimal_modules: at：单次定时投递 Instant
tick：周期定时与 AtomicCell 时间戳
never：始终 Empty 的兜底通道
lectures.practice_task: 用 tick(Duration::from_secs(1)) 实现每秒打印一次的定时器；用 after(Duration::from_secs(5)) 配合 select 给一个长任务加超时。说明 never 在 select 中何时有用。

lectures.title: select 动态选择算法
lectures.topic: 剖析 select.rs 的 Select 动态多路选择：start（try_select 全部）→register（注册阻塞）→decide（等待唤醒）→unregister→accept（完成）的五阶段算法。
learning_goals: 理解 Select 构建操作列表并动态多路选择
掌握五阶段选择算法与公平性保证
认识 select（必须完成）与 ready（仅返回就绪索引）两种模式
minimal_modules: Select 构建 &dyn SelectHandle 列表
run_select：try_select→register→wait_until→unregister→accept
select vs ready，Selected 状态机
lectures.practice_task: 用 Select 在两个 unbounded 通道间做动态选择，打印先到的消息。再实现一个超时版本：加入一个 after(...) 通道作为第三路。对照 select.rs 说明每个操作如何被 register 与 accept。

lectures.title: select! 宏
lectures.topic: 剖析 select_macro.rs 的 select! 宏：解析阶段（@list/@case）与代码生成阶段（@init/@count/@add/@complete），以及编译期优化与容量限制。
learning_goals: 理解声明式宏的分阶段解析与代码生成
掌握 select! 展开后的运行时逻辑与 select.rs 的关系
认识 default 分支与 case 数量上限
minimal_modules: 解析阶段：@list/@list_error/@case 与语法诊断
代码生成：@init 优化/@count/@add/@complete
select! 与底层 Select 的对应
lectures.practice_task: 写一段 select! 同时 recv 两个通道并带 default 超时分支，用 cargo expand（或手动推理）查看展开结果，标注展开代码对应 select.rs 的哪几个阶段。

lectures.title: ArrayQueue：有界 MPMC 队列
lectures.topic: 剖析 array_queue.rs 的有界 MPMC 环形队列：用 lap（幂次）+ index 编码与 slot stamp 防止 ABA，head/tail 的 CAS 推进。
learning_goals: 理解 lap/index 编码与 one_lap 防 ABA 机制
掌握 Slot 的 stamp 状态机（可写/可读）
认识 CachePadded 的 head/tail 避免伪共享
minimal_modules: Slot{stamp,value} 与 lap 编码
push：CAS tail + 写值 + 更新 stamp
pop：CAS head + 读值 + 重置 stamp 为下一圈
lectures.practice_task: 用 ArrayQueue 在 4 生产者 4 消费者下传递消息并验证无丢失。阅读 array_queue.rs 解释：若不用 lap（代次）会出现什么 ABA 问题。

lectures.title: SegQueue：无界分段队列
lectures.topic: 剖析 seg_queue.rs 的无界分段队列：链表式 Block（每块 31 槽）、index 编码带 HAS_NEXT 位、按需分配新块与块回收。
learning_goals: 理解 Block 链表与 BLOCK_CAP=31 的设计
掌握 index 编码（SHIFT/HAS_NEXT/LAP）与 slot 的 WRITE/READ/DESTROY 位
认识段的分配、推进与回收协议
minimal_modules: Block/Slot 布局与状态位
push：预分配下一段 + CAS tail + 写值置 WRITE
pop：CAS head + 等待 WRITE + 读值置 READ + 段回收
lectures.practice_task: 用 SegQueue 做无界传递并持续发送 1e6 条消息。阅读 seg_queue.rs 估算段分配次数，并解释最后一个读取某槽的线程负责释放段的协议。

units.title: crossbeam-epoch：基于 epoch 的内存回收

lectures.title: epoch 内存回收：问题背景与 API
lectures.topic: 引入并发数据结构的内存回收难题，讲解基于 epoch 的回收思想，并概览 crossbeam-epoch 的公共 API（pin/Guard/Collector/Atomic/Shared/Owned）。
learning_goals: 理解无锁数据结构删除节点的安全回收难题
掌握 epoch 回收的基本思想（延迟两个 epoch）
认识 crossbeam-epoch 的核心类型与 pin 入口
minimal_modules: 问题背景：删除与并发读取的竞争
epoch 回收原理：标记→延迟→销毁
公共 API：pin/default_collector/is_pinned 与重导出
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 lib.rs 文档注释，用一段话说明「为什么 GC 语言里无锁结构更简单」。在本地执行 cargo doc -p crossbeam-epoch 浏览 pin/Guard/Atomic 的文档。

lectures.title: Atomic/Shared/Owned 原子指针与标签
lectures.topic: 剖析 atomic.rs：Atomic<T> 原子指针、低地址位标签打包、Owned（独占）与 Shared<'g,T>（借用）的区别，以及 load/store/swap/compare_exchange。
learning_goals: 理解 AtomicPtr + 低地址位标签打包
掌握 Owned vs Shared 的所有权语义与 Pointer trait
认识 compare_exchange 同时比较指针与标签
minimal_modules: Atomic<T> 与 low_bits/compose_tag/decompose_tag
Owned/Shared/Pointer 的关系与转换
load/load_consume/store/swap/compare_exchange
lectures.practice_task: 用 Atomic<T> 存一个带 2 位标签的指针，写一段代码用 compare_exchange 原子地更新指针并修改标签。说明 Shared 的生命周期 'g 为何与 Guard 绑定。

lectures.title: Guard 与 pin 机制
lectures.topic: 剖析 guard.rs 的 RAII Guard：pin 增加保护计数、defer/defer_destroy/defer_unchecked 延迟销毁，以及 deferred.rs 的类型擦除闭包与 default.rs 的全局收集器。
learning_goals: 理解 Guard 的 RAII 与可重入 guard_count
掌握 defer 系列方法的延迟执行语义
认识 Deferred 类型擦除闭包与全局 pin 入口
minimal_modules: Guard RAII + guard_count 可重入 pin
defer/defer_destroy/defer_unchecked
Deferred 内联/装箱闭包与全局 pin()
lectures.practice_task: 用 pin() 获得 Guard，defer_destroy 一段共享数据，观察它在后续 pin 周期后才真正释放（用计数/日志验证）。说明 defer_unchecked 为何是 unsafe。

lectures.title: Collector 与参与者注册
lectures.topic: 剖析 collector.rs 的 Collector（Arc<Global>）与 LocalHandle：参与者注册到全局链表、LocalHandle::pin 的可重入保护与周期性回收。
learning_goals: 理解 Collector 与 LocalHandle 的关系
掌握参与者注册到全局链表的协议
认识 pin 时记录局部 epoch 与 SeqCst fence 的作用
minimal_modules: Collector{Arc<Global>} 与 LocalHandle
register：插入全局参与者链表
LocalHandle::pin：guard_count++/记录 epoch/fence/周期 collect
lectures.practice_task: 创建一个自定义 Collector，register 两个参与者并各自 pin，用日志观察 LocalHandle 的 guard_count 变化。说明为什么需要 fence 保证回收安全。

lectures.title: internal：全局状态、epoch 推进与垃圾回收
lectures.topic: 剖析 internal.rs 的 Global（参与者链表+全局垃圾队列+epoch）与 Local（线程局部 garbage bag），以及 try_advance 推进 epoch、collect 销毁过期垃圾的完整协议。
learning_goals: 理解 Global/Local 的字段布局与协作
掌握 try_advance 的「所有参与者已离开旧 epoch」判定
认识 Bag/SealedBag 的两 epoch 延迟销毁原理
minimal_modules: Global：locals 链表 + queue 垃圾队列 + epoch
try_advance：fence + 检查所有参与者 + 推进
Bag/SealedBag 与 epoch 差≥2 才销毁
lectures.practice_task: 画一张 epoch 推进时序图：参与者 A 在 epoch=0 pin→defer 垃圾→B 推进 epoch→何时该垃圾被销毁。解释为何要等 2 个 epoch 而非 1 个。

lectures.title: 工作窃取模型与公共 API
lectures.topic: 讲解工作窃取调度模型：Injector（全局 FIFO）、Worker（FIFO/LIFO 本地队列）、Stealer（可共享窃取句柄）与 Steal 枚举的 API 与使用范式。
learning_goals: 理解 Injector/Worker/Stealer/Steal 的角色与典型调度结构
掌握 push/pop/steal/steal_batch_and_pop 的用法
认识 Steal::Retry 需要重试的语义
minimal_modules: Worker::new_fifo/new_lifo、stealer()、Injector
steal/steal_batch/steal_batch_and_pop
Steal{Empty,Success,Retry} 与重试模式
lectures.practice_task: 搭建一个迷你工作窃取调度器：1 个 Injector + N 个 Worker，主线程往 Injector 注入任务，工作线程先取本地、再偷同伴、再取 Injector。验证任务全部被执行。

lectures.title: Chase-Lev 双端队列实现
lectures.topic: 深入 deque.rs 的 Chase-Lev 实现：front/back 原子索引、幂次环形 Buffer、FIFO/LIFO 的 push/pop 方向差异、steal 协议与 buffer 扩缩容及 epoch 回收。
learning_goals: 理解 Chase-Lev 的 front/back 索引与环形缓冲
掌握 FIFO vs LIFO 的 pop 方向差异与 steal 协议
认识 buffer resize 与用 epoch 安全回收旧缓冲
minimal_modules: Inner{front,back,buffer} + Buffer 环形与 CachePadded
push（写 back）/pop（FIFO 增 front / LIFO 减 back）/steal（CAS front）
resize 扩缩容与 epoch 延迟回收旧 buffer
lectures.practice_task: 阅读 deque.rs 的 steal()，解释为何 steal 返回 Retry 时应重试而非当作空。构造一个竞争场景：多个 Stealer 同时偷同一个 Worker，观察 Retry/Success/Empty 的分布。

units.title: crossbeam-skiplist 与综合：测试与正确性

lectures.title: 无锁跳表结构
lectures.topic: 剖析 base.rs 的无锁跳表节点结构：refs_and_height 打包引用计数与高度、Tower 多层指针、xorshift 高度生成与 mark_tower 删除标记。
learning_goals: 理解 Node 的 refs_and_height 打包与 Tower 布局
掌握高度生成与 max_height 提示
认识 mark_tower 自顶向下的逻辑删除标记
minimal_modules: Node{value,key,refs_and_height,tower} 布局
xorshift 高度生成与 max_height 提示
引用计数 try_increment/decrement/finalize 与 mark_tower
lectures.practice_task: 画出一个 3 层、4 个节点的跳表示意图，标注每个节点的 tower 指针。阅读 base.rs 说明 mark_tower 为何要从最高层向第 0 层逐层标记。

lectures.title: 跳表操作与 epoch 集成
lectures.topic: 讲解跳表的并发插入/查找/删除（search_bound、协助摘除已删除节点），以及如何用 crossbeam-epoch 的 Guard 延迟释放节点；并介绍 SkipMap/SkipSet 包装。
learning_goals: 理解 search_bound 的逐层下降与协助清理
掌握并发 insert/get/remove 的无锁协调
认识 epoch Guard 在节点释放中的关键作用与 SkipMap/SkipSet 接口
minimal_modules: search_bound 逐层下降 + 协助 unlink
并发 insert/remove 与引用计数收尾
epoch guard.defer_unchecked 释放节点 + Map/Set 包装
lectures.practice_task: 用 SkipMap 在多线程并发 insert/get/remove，验证最终状态一致。阅读 base.rs 找到所有 defer_unchecked 调用点，说明每处释放的对象与触发时机。

lectures.title: 测试、loom 与并发正确性
lectures.topic: 总结 crossbeam 如何保证并发正确性：cargo test、miri（UB 检测）、tsan/san（数据竞争与 sanitizer）、loom（模型检查）与 CI 流水线及基准对比。
learning_goals: 理解 loom 状态空间模型检查的原理
掌握 miri/tsan/san 在并发代码中的作用
认识 CI 流水线与 benchmarks 的工程意义
minimal_modules: loom：crossbeam_loom cfg 与内部 primitive 抽象
miri/san/tsan：UB 与数据竞争检测
CI 流水线与 benchmarks 性能对比
lectures.practice_task: 在 crossbeam-epoch 上运行 ci/crossbeam-epoch-loom.sh（或 cargo +nightly miri test -p crossbeam-utils）观察输出。阅读 benchmarks 的 run.sh，说明 crossbeam 与其它通道实现的对比维度。
